sessions.intent: Design ISettings pattern — strongly typed, per-module, goal-scoped settings with source generator support

before.assumptions: Data.Envelope.cs on data-envelope-architecture branch has hardcoded 100MB gzip bomb limit. No settings mechanism exists in Runtime2 for action/module configuration.
before.risk: New pattern touching source generator, context scoping, engine navigation, and builder discovery. Broad surface but well-contained design.

sessions.plan: ISettings design: each module declares a strongly typed settings class implementing ISettings. Source generator produces scope-aware property bodies (read), settings action handler (write), and builder registry. Settings are goal-scoped with Default flag for engine persistence. Navigation: engine.Module<Archive>().Settings.Max. First use case: replace hardcoded gzip bomb limit.

actions.context: Full design spec for ISettings pattern — phases, scoping, source generator, open questions for coder
Session summary and cross-session summary

after.status: Design complete, ready for coder handoff
after.health: Clean — no code changes, design only
after.notes: Builder integration (discovery action, LLM prompt design) deferred. Depends on data-envelope-architecture branch for Data.Envelope.cs and Module<T> pattern.

sessions.intent: Scaffold ISettings type skeletons and failing tests for strongly typed, goal-scoped module settings

before.assumptions: Architect plan exists in .bot/compression-settings/architect/v1/plan.md. No settings infrastructure exists in Runtime2 yet. No Module<T>() pattern, no SettingsScope, no ISettings. data-envelope-architecture branch (with Archive module) does not exist on this branch — archive use case will be skeleton-only.
before.risk: New cross-cutting feature touching Engine, PLangContext, source generator, and a new action module. Large surface area but well-defined by architect plan.

sessions.plan: Translate architect ISettings design into compilable type skeletons and failing tests. Phase 1-3: ISettings marker interface, Scope (key-value store), Settings @this (registry + resolution), ModuleView<T> (context-bound view). Add Settings to Engine, SettingsScope to PLangContext, push/pop in Goal.RunAsync. Archive module as first use case. 15 failing tests (C# + PLang). Departed from architect: Engine.Settings owns resolution behavior (OBP), not PLangContext. No Module<T>() on Engine (premature).

actions.context: Core settings infrastructure — marker interface, scope store, registry with resolution, context-bound view
Wire settings into Engine (property), PLangContext (scope storage), Goal runner (push/pop), global aliases
First use case — archive module settings class, action handler, result types
15 failing C# tests covering scope resolution, inheritance, defaults, thread safety
PLang integration test — will fully pass only after builder integration

after.status: Scaffolding complete — 7 new files, 4 modified, 15 failing tests. PLang builds clean. Ready for coder.
after.health: Green — 0 new compilation errors. All tests fail with NotImplementedException (red phase).
after.notes: Test project has 148 pre-existing errors (not from this work). Source generator changes (Phase 2) deferred to coder.

sessions.intent: Fix 148 test compilation errors — Engine namespace shadowing global alias

before.assumptions: The 148 errors are all CS0118: 'Engine' is a namespace but is used like a type. Root cause: global using alias Engine = PLang.Runtime2.Engine.@this is shadowed by namespace PLang.Runtime2.Engine when test files import sub-namespaces.
before.risk: Mass rename across 35 files. Low risk — mechanical replacement of type reference, no logic changes.

sessions.plan: Replace all uses of Engine as a type (constructor calls, field declarations, parameters) with fully-qualified PLang.Runtime2.Engine.@this across all affected test files. Also fix TUnit await assertion errors in Settings test files and archive namespace resolution in ModuleViewTests.

actions.context: Replace Engine type with PLang.Runtime2.Engine.@this in core test files
Replace Engine type with PLang.Runtime2.Engine.@this in module test files
Replace Engine type with PLang.Runtime2.Engine.@this in other test files
Fix TUnit await assertions, fix archive namespace reference via using alias

after.status: All 148 errors fixed. Build: 0 errors, 87 warnings. Tests: 1239 pass, 15 fail (all NotImplementedException from Settings skeletons — expected red phase).
after.health: Green — clean build, all pre-existing tests passing
after.notes: Root cause: global using alias shadowed by namespace. Fix: fully-qualified PLang.Runtime2.Engine.@this. This is a pre-existing issue that affects any test file importing Engine sub-namespaces.

sessions.intent: Implement Settings method bodies — make 15 red tests green

before.assumptions: Scaffolder created all types with NotImplementedException bodies. 15 tests define the spec. Tests are the contract.
before.risk: Low — straightforward dictionary/scope-chain logic. No architectural decisions needed.

sessions.plan: Implement Scope (ConcurrentDictionary wrapper), @this.Resolve (walk context→parent→defaults→classDefault), @this.Set (isDefault→Defaults, else→context.SettingsScope), @this.For (namespace→prefix, new ModuleView), ModuleView.Resolve (prefix+delegate).

actions.context: Replaced all 6 NotImplementedException bodies with working implementations. Scope: 3 one-liners. Settings: Resolve walks scope chain, Set writes to context or defaults, For derives module prefix from namespace. ModuleView: prefixes and delegates.

after.status: All 15 settings tests green. 1254 total tests pass, 0 failures.
after.notes: Clean implementation — no surprises. Tests were well-specified.

sessions.intent: Code simplicity analysis of runtime2-settings branch — OBP compliance, simplification, readability

before.assumptions: Branch adds ISettings, Settings, Scope, ModuleView, archive.Settings. Previous bots covered scaffolding and implementation. No simplicity review done yet.

sessions.plan: Analyze all new/changed production files (Settings subsystem + Engine/Context diffs + archive.Settings) and test files. Three-pass: OBP, simplification, readability.

actions.context: Full 3-pass analysis. 10 production files, all CLEAN. No OBP violations. One medium finding: hard cast in Resolve<T>.
Readability pass. 4 test files, all CLEAN. Good scope chain coverage.
Analysis report and summaries.

after.status: Analysis complete. 14 CLEAN, 0 NEEDS WORK. No OBP violations. 3 findings (1 medium, 1 low, 1 info). Post-analysis: reviewed tester v1 findings and recorded 5 learnings.
after.health: Code is clean, minimal, and OBP-compliant.
after.notes: Most actionable: hard cast (T)value in Resolve<T> — one-line fix. Tester correctly elevated this to CRITICAL (C# unboxing + JSON deserialization). Learned to add test-gap analysis to my passes.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of coder v1 Settings infrastructure — hunt for false greens and missing coverage

before.assumptions: Coder v1 implemented 6 method bodies. 15 C# tests and 1 PLang test exist. Code analyzer found all files CLEAN with one medium finding (hard cast).
before.risk: Low — analysis only, no code changes.

sessions.plan: Run full test suite. Read all source and test files. Analyze test quality: false greens, missing coverage, weak assertions, edge cases. Cross-reference code analyzer findings. Write test-report.json.

actions.context: Deep test quality analysis. Found 6 issues: 1 critical (hard cast false green), 2 major (goal save/restore untested, scope chain gap untested), 3 minor.
Tester report and summaries.
Added two todos: set up PLang test environment for bots, bot reminder system.

after.status: Test review complete. Verdict: needs-fixes. 6 findings (1 critical, 2 major, 3 minor).
after.health: Tests are green but have gaps — the critical hard cast finding means the first real production use could crash.
after.notes: PLang tests could not be run — no plang binary or OpenAI key in environment. Added todo to fix.

sessions.intent: Fix hard cast in Resolve<T>, add 5 missing tests from tester/analyzer reports

before.assumptions: Tester found critical hard cast false green, 2 major missing coverage gaps, and 3 minor issues. Analyzer confirmed the hard cast finding.
before.risk: Low — Convert.ChangeType handles numeric widening. Tests are additive.

sessions.plan: Replace (T)value with Cast<T> helper using is/Convert.ChangeType/fallback. Add tests: int→long widening, type mismatch fallback, 3-level scope chain gap, goal save/restore isolation, scope overwrite.

actions.context: Added Cast<T> helper: tries 'is T', then Convert.ChangeType, then fallback. Handles int→long widening and type mismatches gracefully.
5 new tests: int→long widening, type mismatch fallback, 3-level null scope gap, goal save/restore isolation, scope overwrite.

after.status: All findings addressed. 1259 tests pass, 0 failures.
after.notes: Tester finding #5 (null value on Set) left as-is — ConcurrentDictionary's ArgumentNullException is correct behavior for a settings store. Finding #6 (more PLang tests) deferred — requires builder.

sessions.intent: Re-review coder v2 fixes — verify all v1 findings are properly addressed

before.assumptions: Coder v2 fixed Cast<T>, added 8 tests (widening, enum, mismatch, gap, save/restore, overwrite, null removal, clone). Code analyzer contributed enum and clone fixes.

sessions.plan: Run tests. Read all new/changed code and tests. Verify each v1 finding is properly resolved. Check for new issues introduced by fixes.

actions.context: Verified all v1 findings resolved. Two minor items remain (simulation test, PLang tests). Verdict: approved.
Updated test report and summaries.

after.status: Approved. All critical/major findings resolved. 1262 tests pass.
after.notes: Two minor deferred items: GoalRunAsync integration test (needs infrastructure), PLang tests (needs builder).

sessions.intent: Security audit of Settings infrastructure — blue/red team analysis

before.assumptions: Settings adds ~200 LOC of new production code. Previous bots (architect, scaffolder, coder, tester, codeanalyzer) have reviewed functionality and test quality. No security review done yet.
before.risk: Low — small feature, well-isolated, all inputs from trusted .pr files.

sessions.plan: Read all new/changed code. Map attack surface (resolution, write, security control weakening, module view). Red team: attempt exploitation. Write security-report.json with findings.

actions.context: Blue/red team security audit. 4 attack surface areas mapped. 4 findings (1 medium, 3 low), all accepted-risk under user-sovereign threat model.
Security report and summaries.

after.status: Audit complete. No critical or high findings. 4 low/medium findings, all accepted-risk.
after.health: Good — Settings infrastructure is well-designed with proper isolation, thread safety, and defensive type conversion.
after.notes: Future watch: if settings become writable from untrusted sources, add value validation and dictionary size limits.

sessions.intent: Auditor code review of Settings infrastructure — OBP, contracts, test quality, ripple impact

before.assumptions: Coder v2 fixed all tester findings. Tester v2 approved. Security audit done (4 accepted-risk). ~200 LOC production, 23 tests, 1262 pass. No auditor review yet.
before.risk: Low — small, well-isolated feature. Review only.

sessions.plan: Read all coder/tester/security summaries. Read all production code and tests. Run test suite. Check OBP compliance, contract integrity, clone semantics, scope chain correctness. Write auditor-report.json with findings.

actions.context: Full auditor review. OBP clean. 4 findings: 1 major (Clone shares Scope by reference), 2 minor (save/restore complexity, simulation test), 1 nit (bare catch).
Test quality review. Tests are honest — good scope chain coverage. Clone test only tests reading, not write isolation.
Auditor report and summaries.

after.status: Approved with one fix recommended. Clone() shares Scope by reference — should deep-clone before merge.
after.health: Clean infrastructure. OBP compliant. One contract bug (Clone isolation).
after.notes: Finding #1 is the only one that should block merge. Minor findings are future-proofing suggestions.

sessions.intent: Fix auditor major finding: Clone() shares SettingsScope by reference; narrow Cast<T> bare catch

before.assumptions: Auditor found major contract bug: Clone() copies SettingsScope by reference, so mutations in clone pollute original. Security/tester flagged bare catch in Cast<T>.
before.risk: Low — targeted fixes to 3 files, additive tests.

sessions.plan: 1. Add Scope.Clone() method (new ConcurrentDictionary from existing). 2. PLangContext.Clone() uses SettingsScope?.Clone(). 3. Narrow Cast<T> catch to InvalidCastException/FormatException/OverflowException. 4. Add 3 tests: Scope clone isolation, empty scope clone, context clone write isolation.

actions.context: Added Clone() method — creates new Scope with independent ConcurrentDictionary snapshot.
Changed SettingsScope = SettingsScope to SettingsScope = SettingsScope?.Clone() in Clone().
Narrowed bare catch to catch (Exception ex) when (ex is InvalidCastException or FormatException or OverflowException).
3 new tests: Scope clone isolation (bidirectional), empty scope clone, context clone write isolation.

after.status: All auditor/security findings addressed. 1265 tests pass, 0 failures.
after.notes: Major contract bug fixed. Clone now produces fully independent contexts.

sessions.intent: Re-review coder v3 fixes — verify auditor/security findings addressed, check for regressions

before.assumptions: Coder v3 fixed Clone isolation and narrowed Cast<T> catch. Auditor approved with one fix. Security found 4 accepted-risk items.
before.risk: Low — review only. But narrowing catch clauses can introduce regressions.

sessions.plan: Run tests. Read all changed code and new tests. Verify auditor findings resolved. Check narrowed catch for gaps. Write test-report.json.

actions.context: Clone isolation resolved. Found regression: narrowed catch misses ArgumentException from Enum.ToObject on string→enum path.
Tester report and summaries.

after.status: Needs-fixes. Narrowed catch introduced regression: string→enum crashes with unhandled ArgumentException.
after.health: One regression path. Clone isolation is solid.
after.notes: One-line fix: add ArgumentException to catch filter, or use Enum.TryParse for string→enum.
